title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Two Boston men, Anthony Shearrion and Arkie Swinson, were charged with assault after an altercation over a parking accident.
The incident occurred near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester, where the suspects allegedly followed and beat the victim.
The suspects were arraigned and held at Suffolk County Jail, pending a court date on April 14.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox return to the postseason aims to improve their lineup and bullpen under manager Alex Cora.
Roman Anthony will lead the Red Sox offense, but concerns linger over run scoring and offensive depth.
Infield defense and managing a crowded outfield are key issues for Boston entering the 2026 season.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced 'Match-Ready Massachusetts,' a public safety initiative for the 2026 World Cup in Foxborough.
The initiative is funded by $76 million in federal funds, focusing on cybersecurity, emergency preparedness, and transportation.
Boston City Hall Plaza will host an official FIFA Fan Festival featuring live broadcasts and local vendor offerings.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Kraft-owned New England Revolution supports the White Stadium renovation in Franklin Park, likening it to its Everett stadium proposal.
Boston's White Stadium renovation involves a public-private partnership and faces legal challenges, with opponents citing Massachusetts constitutional concerns.
Boston Legacy FC's involvement requires $252 million in community benefits over 15 years, marking the city's largest community benefit agreement.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Medford man was critically injured on I-95 in Veazie, Maine after stopping to aid a stranded driver.
The accident occurred when a 19-year-old driver lost control in snowy conditions, striking the man and a stranded 18-year-old woman.
Police suspect the man was trying to shield the woman; both drivers involved suffered minor injuries and were hospitalized.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Monterey resident John Rohane arrested following a suspicious fire at his home on March 25.
No injuries reported in the early-morning fire, which is believed to have been intentionally set.
Rohane was found by police in wooded areas and taken to a hospital for unrelated issues.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Falmouth Fire and Rescue crews saved an 11-year-old Labrador using pepperoni to coax her from a 12-foot sinkhole.
The sinkhole in the dog's backyard was caused by an underground pipe leak, leading to soil erosion beneath.
Rescuers avoided entering the unstable hole and used a pole leash to safely extract the uninjured dog.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue took in 16 neglected axolotls from an overcrowding situation, seeking donations for their rehabilitation.
The axolotls, named after cheeses, suffer from missing limbs and bacterial infections, with some already adopted.
Libertyland, formed in 2024, focuses on axolotl education, rehabilitation, and adoption through a New England foster network.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: A24 is hosting a free preview screening of 'The Drama' starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson in Somerville.
A Love Letters podcast episode discusses imperfections in objects symbolizing personal growth and relationship lessons.
Readers are encouraged to share photos of personal objects with imperfections that represent community, love, or lessons.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School launches a student-run salon with 25 stations to provide real-world cosmetology training.
The salon offers hair, skin, and nail services to the public under instructor supervision, enhancing training realism.
Students can accumulate 1,000 training hours and qualify for the state licensing exam upon graduation.
